title: Kimai vs TrackingTime for Power users
meta_description: Compare Kimai vs TrackingTime for power users. Kimai wins when you need self-hosted control over your time tracking infrastructure.
constraint_lens: Power users need tools that offer maximum control and flexibility over their data and infrastructure.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for power users who need full control over their time tracking infrastructure.
one_second_verdict.reason: TrackingTime fails first because it is limited to a hosted SaaS system without self-hosting control.

verdict.summary: Kimai is the better choice when you need full ownership and control over your tracking system. It can be self-hosted, allowing customization, data control, and infrastructure flexibility. TrackingTime is a hosted SaaS tool, which limits control over deployment, data, and customization.
verdict.decision_rule: If tracking time is limited to a hosted SaaS system without self-hosting control, TrackingTime fails first.

sections.heading: Why Kimai fits this power user better
sections.content: This user needs control over how the system is hosted and managed. Kimai supports that by allowing self-hosting, giving full ownership of data, customization, and infrastructure.

bullets.point: Kimai can be self-hosted on your own infrastructure.
bullets.why_it_matters: You have full control over deployment, data, and system configuration.

bullets.point: The system can be customized and extended beyond standard SaaS limitations.
bullets.why_it_matters: This allows deeper flexibility and long-term scalability.

bullets.point: Data is fully owned and controlled by the user.
bullets.why_it_matters: This is critical for users who need control over privacy and infrastructure.

bullets.point: TrackingTime is a hosted SaaS tool that requires no setup or infrastructure management.
bullets.why_it_matters: This reduces effort, but limits control.

bullets.point: The system is ready to use without deployment or maintenance.
bullets.why_it_matters: This simplifies onboarding, but sacrifices flexibility.

bullets.point: Updates and hosting are managed by the provider.
bullets.why_it_matters: This removes responsibility, but restricts customization.

sections.heading: Where each tool can break down

items.fails_when: You do not want to manage hosting, deployment, or system maintenance.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use TrackingTime if you prefer a fully managed SaaS solution.

items.fails_when: You need full control over hosting, data, and system customization.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to Kimai to self-host and control your tracking infrastructure.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This can flip if the user prioritizes convenience and does not need control over hosting or infrastructure. In that case, TrackingTime may be more suitable.

sections.heading: Quick rules
rules: Choose Kimai if you need self-hosting and control.
Choose TrackingTime if you want a managed SaaS tool.

faqs.q: Why is Kimai better for control?
faqs.a: Because it can be self-hosted, giving full ownership over data and infrastructure.
It is a hosted SaaS tool without self-hosting options.
Yes, because it does not require setup or maintenance.

faqs.q: What is the best tool for self-hosted time tracking?
faqs.a: Kimai is better because it provides full control over the system.
